prd_user_segments.name: Power Users
prd_user_segments.description: High-frequency users who engage with advanced features daily and drive most of the product's word-of-mouth growth
prd_user_segments.primary_jobs_to_be_done: Automate complex workflows and get real-time visibility into business data without switching tools

prd_user_segments.name: New Signups
prd_user_segments.description: Users within their first 30 days who are evaluating whether the product solves their problem
prd_user_segments.primary_jobs_to_be_done: Understand if the product can replace their current solution quickly enough to justify switching

prd_user_segments.name: Team Admins
prd_user_segments.description: Users managing workspace settings, billing, and team access for 5+ members
prd_user_segments.primary_jobs_to_be_done: Maintain control over data access and ensure the team is using the product effectively without manual oversight

prd_user_segments.name: Occasional Users
prd_user_segments.description: Users who log in less than once per week, typically checking outputs produced by other tools or team members
prd_user_segments.primary_jobs_to_be_done: Spot-check data or review reports prepared by others without investing time in learning the full product

prd_sprints.name: Sprint 41
prd_sprints.goal: Ship the pipeline monitoring dashboard and fix top 3 customer-reported data display bugs
prd_sprints.retrospective_notes: Underestimated the dashboard complexity. Dashboard shipped but one bug fix rolled over. Velocity holding steady at 46-50 points.

prd_sprints.name: Sprint 42
prd_sprints.goal: Launch segment-based experiment targeting and complete the onboarding flow redesign

prd_sprints.name: Sprint 40
prd_sprints.goal: Migrate the routing engine to the new worker architecture and ship CSV export
prd_sprints.retrospective_notes: Migration scope grew significantly during sprint. CSV export shipped. 10-point rollover caused by unexpected worker compatibility issue discovered mid-sprint.

prd_features.title: Segment-Based Experiment Targeting
prd_features.description: Allow experiment operators to target A/B tests at specific user segments instead of a random traffic split across all users

prd_features.title: CSV Export for All Entity Types
prd_features.description: Add one-click CSV export to every entity list view, respecting column visibility settings and active filters

prd_features.title: Onboarding Flow Redesign
prd_features.description: Redesign the 7-step onboarding wizard into a 3-step guided setup based on user research showing high drop-off at step 4

prd_features.title: Pipeline Health Dashboard
prd_features.description: Consolidated real-time dashboard showing pipeline status, latency, error rates, and recent anomalies across all active pipelines

prd_experiments.name: Onboarding Step Reduction
prd_experiments.hypothesis: We believe reducing the onboarding wizard from 7 steps to 3 steps will increase 7-day activation rate for New Signups because users are dropping off at step 4 before completing setup

prd_experiments.name: In-App Upgrade Prompt Timing
prd_experiments.hypothesis: We believe showing the upgrade prompt after a user creates their 5th record (instead of on day 14) will increase free-to-paid conversion because users hit the value moment before seeing pricing
prd_experiments.control_variant: Upgrade prompt shown on day 14 of trial
prd_experiments.test_variant: Upgrade prompt shown after 5th record created

prd_experiments.name: Empty State Call-to-Action Copy
prd_experiments.hypothesis: We believe changing empty state CTA copy from 'Get Started' to 'Create your first pipeline' will increase empty state click-through rate because specific copy outperforms generic copy

prd_research_sessions.research_question: Where do users get stuck in the current 7-step onboarding wizard?
key_findings: 6 of 8 participants abandoned at step 4 (source configuration) without seeking help
Step 4 requires knowledge of database credentials that users did not have on hand
pain_points_identified: No way to save progress and return later if credentials are unavailable
Error messages at step 4 are technical and do not suggest a recovery action
The progress indicator shows 7 steps which makes the wizard feel long before starting

prd_research_sessions.research_question: What would make Power Users recommend the product more actively to peers?
key_findings: Primary blocker to referral is lack of a dedicated referral or team invite flow
Power Users want to share specific views or dashboards with external stakeholders without giving full access
3 of 5 participants mentioned wanting a mobile app for monitoring alerts on the go
pain_points_identified: No guest access or read-only sharing link for external stakeholders
Alert notifications only available via email - no push notification support
